title: ranunculus aquatilis var. capillaceus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis var. capillaceus, also known as the White Water Crowfoot, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. The health benefits of White Water Crowfoot include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of White Water Crowfoot can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support skin health and reduce inflammation.
